task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly calculates the trimmed mean by excluding the minimum and maximum values, but could be more concise in its calculation of the total.
trace: The function first checks if the list is empty and returns None in that case. Then, it sorts the given list and skips handling the edge cases where there is only one element in the list by checking the length of the sorted list. It then iterates through the sorted list, excluding the minimum and maximum elements from the total sum. Finally, it calculates and returns the trimmed mean as the total sum divided by the number of remaining elements (excluding the minimum and maximum). This function handles an edge case where there is only one element in the list.
